description: Default destructive-app denylist for SP-063-4h. Categories: financial, system, destructive, password_manager. Override at ~/.config/sprout/computer_use_denylist_overrides.json (override wins on conflict). See roadmap/SP-063-destructive-denylist-design.md.

macos.reason: Send button in Mail sends email to recipients.
Erase button formats a disk.
System Settings can change security / network / privacy settings.
Terminal with destructive commands in title can run privileged or destructive actions.
iTerm2 with destructive commands in title can run privileged or destructive actions.
Password manager — auto-fill could leak credentials.
Password manager (1Password 8+) — auto-fill could leak credentials.
Keychain Access — exposes stored credentials.
Bitwarden password manager — auto-fill could leak credentials.
LastPass password manager — auto-fill could leak credentials.
Bank of America desktop app — financial transactions reachable by clicks.
Chase desktop app — financial transactions reachable by clicks.
Wells Fargo desktop app — financial transactions reachable by clicks.
PayPal desktop app — financial transactions reachable by clicks.
Coinbase Pro desktop app — financial transactions reachable by clicks.
Safari in incognito mode — typed URLs aren't logged, harder to audit mistakes.
Chrome in incognito mode — typed URLs aren't logged, harder to audit mistakes.
Firefox in private browsing mode — typed URLs aren't logged, harder to audit mistakes.
Edge in InPrivate mode — typed URLs aren't logged, harder to audit mistakes.
Brave in incognito mode — typed URLs aren't logged, harder to audit mistakes.
Finder with destructive action in title — empty trash / permanent delete.

linux.reason: Thunderbird compose window — Send button sends email.
Evolution compose window — Send button sends email.
GNOME Disks erase / format dialog.
Polkit authentication agent — password prompt for privileged actions.
KDE Polkit authentication agent — password prompt for privileged actions.
PAM password prompt — type into a password field.
SSH passphrase prompt — typing here leaks the passphrase.
GNOME keyring / GPG passphrase prompt — typing here leaks the passphrase.
GPG pinentry prompt — typing here leaks the passphrase.
KeePassXC password manager — auto-fill could leak credentials.
Bitwarden password manager — auto-fill could leak credentials.
1Password password manager — auto-fill could leak credentials.
LastPass password manager — auto-fill could leak credentials.
Chrome in incognito mode — typed URLs aren't logged, harder to audit mistakes.
Firefox in private browsing mode — typed URLs aren't logged, harder to audit mistakes.
Chromium in incognito mode — typed URLs aren't logged, harder to audit mistakes.
Edge in InPrivate mode — typed URLs aren't logged, harder to audit mistakes.
Brave in incognito mode — typed URLs aren't logged, harder to audit mistakes.
Terminal emulator with destructive command in title — sudo, rm -rf, dd, mkfs all reachable by Enter.
File manager with destructive action in title — delete permanently or empty trash.
System Settings can change security / network / privacy settings.
Generic authentication prompt — even non-classified apps become destructive when prompting for a password. Catch-all for ad-hoc shell scripts / custom dialogs.
